description: China's deepening WTO integration drove rapid export growth and global supply chain shifts.
Political upheavals across MENA region disrupted oil production and regional economies.
Crude oil prices fell from over $100 to below $50 per barrel, impacting oil-exporting economies.
Major sell-off in Chinese equities causing global market volatility.
UK voted to leave the EU, causing currency depreciation and trade uncertainty.
Escalating tariffs between US and China disrupted global trade flows.
Global pandemic causing widespread economic disruption.
Post-pandemic supply chain bottlenecks caused shortages and price increases worldwide.
War impacting global energy and food markets.
High inflation rates across many major economies.
Major central banks began cutting interest rates after prolonged tightening cycle.
